depression.description: I feel overwhelmed at the thought of going back to work after my time off. The anxiety about how my coworkers and HR will perceive me is really weighing on me.
Even though I had valid reasons for my absence, I can't shake the guilt. I keep thinking that others will judge me for needing this time away.
I remember a time when HR made me feel judged for taking disability leave, and now I'm terrified that it will happen again. I feel trapped in this cycle of fear.
Sometimes I feel like I'm unlovable, especially when I think about how my absence might affect my relationships at work. It's hard to believe that anyone would care about my well-being.
Running into a coworker while I'm out sick makes me anxious. I worry they'll think I'm faking it or that I'm not really sick, and that just adds to my stress.

Delusion.topic: Need for control in my life
Delusion.description: I always feel like I need to control my situation, but the reality is that I can't. This feeling of helplessness is really frustrating.
I struggle with the belief that if I take care of my health, others will judge me negatively. It feels like I'm stuck between needing to heal and fearing what others think.
